name: When Visual Evidence is Ambiguous: Pareidolia as a Diagnostic Probe for Vision Models
summary: arXiv:2603.03989v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: When visual evidence is ambiguous, vision models must decide how to interpret face-like patterns. Face pareidolia, the perception of faces...
description: arXiv:2603.03989v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: When visual evidence is ambiguous, vision models must decide how to interpret face-like patterns. Face pareidolia, the perception of faces in non-face objects, provides a controlled probe of such decisions. We introduce a diagnostic framework that analyzes detection, localization, uncertainty and bias across class, difficulty and emotion...
author: Qianpu Chen, Derya Soydaner, Rob Saunders
